text: The surgeon lowers the view slightly to expose the dry fat tissue. The appendix is visible, appearing red with some pus present. The structure resembling the appendix trunk is observed.
The surgeon makes a small incision and proceeds to dry the area close to the incision site, avoiding the surrounding fat tissue.
The surgeon inspects the surgical site for the presence of pus or lichen, which can lead to abscess formation if not removed. The surgeon prepares to wash the area before completing the incision.
